title: 1.4 Contributions of Biological Discoveries to Society and the Environment
description: Comprehensive coverage of how biological discoveries, including the microscope, medical discoveries, and genetics, have impacted medicine, ecosystem study, forensic science, atomic study, genetic study, and tissue analysis.

questions.question: Which invention and curative medication are mentioned as having transformed human life?
What is a microscope?
options: A device that magnifies objects or organisms too small to be seen with the naked eye
A device that records electrical activity
questions.correctAnswer: A device that magnifies objects or organisms too small to be seen with the naked eye

questions.question: The microscope has had an enormous influence on the development of which sciences?
options: Modern medical, forensics, and environmental sciences
questions.correctAnswer: Modern medical, forensics, and environmental sciences

questions.question: When did the use of microscopes in medicine begin?
Who reported that microscopic organisms seen through a microscope caused certain diseases?
What did people believe caused diseases before Pasteur's germ theory?
options: Poor diet and lack of exercise

questions.question: What did Pasteur's germ theory revolutionize?
options: The process of identifying, treating, and preventing infectious diseases
questions.correctAnswer: The process of identifying, treating, and preventing infectious diseases

questions.question: How do hospital laboratories use microscopes today according to the text?
options: To identify which microbe is causing an infection so physicians can prescribe the proper medicine
questions.correctAnswer: To identify which microbe is causing an infection so physicians can prescribe the proper medicine

questions.question: Besides identifying microbes, what else are microscopes used for in medicine?
options: To diagnose cancer and other diseases
questions.correctAnswer: To diagnose cancer and other diseases

questions.question: How do field biologists use microscopes to study an ecosystem?
options: By identifying the types and numbers of microscopic organisms found in ecosystem samples
questions.correctAnswer: By identifying the types and numbers of microscopic organisms found in ecosystem samples

questions.question: How does studying microscopic organisms in an ecosystem help scientists?
options: It helps them predict the weather
It helps in defining the ecosystem, detecting threats, and determining relationships of organisms with their environments
It helps them locate mineral deposits
It helps them measure air pollution
questions.correctAnswer: It helps in defining the ecosystem, detecting threats, and determining relationships of organisms with their environments

questions.question: What is forensic science?
options: A field of science used to develop new medicines
A field of science used to gather and analyze evidence to establish facts used in a legal scenario
A field of science used to study ecosystems
A field of science used to classify organisms
questions.correctAnswer: A field of science used to gather and analyze evidence to establish facts used in a legal scenario

questions.question: What can be examined under a microscope to see if bullets match a particular gun?
What type of microscope has enabled scientists to study cells at an atomic level?
What does the atomic force microscope enable scientists to do with viruses?
options: Scrutinize them at their atomic level and influence them for the delivery of innovative treatments
questions.correctAnswer: Scrutinize them at their atomic level and influence them for the delivery of innovative treatments

questions.question: What does the electron microscope enable scientists to study and understand about viruses?
options: The types of viruses and how they infect the body
questions.correctAnswer: The types of viruses and how they infect the body

questions.question: How has the microscope impacted genetics research?
options: It has had a significant impact on genetics research
It has had minimal impact on genetics research
It is not used in genetics research
It is only used in plant genetics
questions.correctAnswer: It has had a significant impact on genetics research

questions.question: What do scientists use a microscope to examine in genetics research?
What does the use of microscopes in genetics allow scientists to evaluate?
options: Genetic abnormalities, regeneration, and tissue death
questions.correctAnswer: Genetic abnormalities, regeneration, and tissue death

questions.question: What will histologists be able to explore as a result of genetics research using microscopes?
What do histologists commonly study using a microscope?
When a section of tissue is taken for analysis, what can histologists determine using a microscope?
Who discovered the circulatory system and in what year?
options: Ibn al-Nafis in 1242, and first brought to prominence in 1628 by William Harvey
questions.correctAnswer: Ibn al-Nafis in 1242, and first brought to prominence in 1628 by William Harvey

questions.question: Who discovered vaccination, and in what year and place?
Who discovered cell theory and in what year?
options: Theodor Schwann and Matthias Schleiden in 1838
questions.correctAnswer: Theodor Schwann and Matthias Schleiden in 1838

questions.question: Who discovered the germ theory and in what year?
Who discovered X-rays and in what year?
Who discovered vitamins and in what period?
Who discovered insulin and in what year?
Who discovered penicillin and in what year?
Who discovered DNA and in what period?
options: James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953
Swiss chemist Friedrich Miescher in the 1860s and 1870s
questions.correctAnswer: Swiss chemist Friedrich Miescher in the 1860s and 1870s

questions.question: Who discovered anesthetics and what was the year of first clinical use?
options: Sigmund Freud and Karl Koller with first clinical use in 1884; Albert Niemann extracted it from leaves of Coca in 1860
questions.correctAnswer: Sigmund Freud and Karl Koller with first clinical use in 1884; Albert Niemann extracted it from leaves of Coca in 1860

questions.question: Who discovered HIV and in what period?
options: Robert Gallo and Luc Montagnier in the 1980s
questions.correctAnswer: Robert Gallo and Luc Montagnier in the 1980s

questions.question: Before 1900, the history of genetics was related to which field?
Who elaborated various smaller things in a thinly sliced cork section from a mature tree?
In 1831, who discovered a consistent structure present in a regular way, called the nucleus?
Who presented cell theory in 1836-1840 to depict various structures present in the cell and their nature?
In 1884-1885, which four scientists derived that heredity matter existed in the nucleus in the material that makes up chromosomes?
What event caused genetics to take a progressive turn and revolutionize?
options: The discovery of Mendel's work and his laws
questions.correctAnswer: The discovery of Mendel's work and his laws

questions.question: According to the text, genetics is providing numerous services to humanity in almost every field of life.
options: True, its prominent role is in the welfare and betterment of human lives
False, genetics is still a theoretical field
questions.correctAnswer: True, its prominent role is in the welfare and betterment of human lives

questions.question: The invention of which device has revolutionized the science industry according to the text?
What did Albert Niemann extract from leaves of Coca in 1860?
According to the text, genetics research using microscopes allows scientists to examine infected tissue for what purpose?
options: To explore future therapies and preventive drugs
questions.correctAnswer: To explore future therapies and preventive drugs

questions.question: Which scientist reported in the 1860s that microscopic organisms caused certain diseases, revolutionizing medicine?
The circulatory system discovery was first brought to prominence in 1628 by which physician?
